name: Mesa (Agent-Based Modeling Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Project Mesa / George Mason University

performance.notes: Pure Python; performance scales linearly with agent count; ~10K agents typical

agent_readiness.notes: Agent-based modeling framework in Python. from mesa import Agent, Model. from mesa.space import MultiGrid. from mesa.time import RandomActivation. from mesa.datacollection import DataCollector. class MoneyAgent(Agent): def step(self): ... class MoneyModel(Model): def __init__(self, n, width, height): self.grid = MultiGrid(width, height, True); self.schedule = RandomActivation(self). for i in range(n): agent = MoneyAgent(i, self); self.grid.place_agent(agent, ...); self.schedule.add(agent). def step(self): self.schedule.step(). Mesa 2.x: AgentSet API. Visualization: SolaraViz. Batch run: BatchRunner.

gotchas: Mesa 2.0 changed API significantly from 1.x — old tutorials don't work
Performance degrades sharply with >100K agents — use Rust/C++ for scale
No built-in parallelism — single-threaded by default
Visualization requires separate install (SolaraViz for Mesa 2.x)
best_when: Social science and economics agent-based models
Epidemiological SIR/SEIR models with agent heterogeneity
Academic/research ABM where Python ecosystem is preferred
avoid_when: Large-scale ABMs with >100K agents (use NetLogo or custom Rust/C++)
summary: Mesa is the standard Python agent-based modeling framework. Agent/Model subclasses. MultiGrid spatial structure. RandomActivation scheduler. DataCollector for metrics. BatchRunner for parameter sweeps. Apache-2.0 by Project Mesa. Mesa 2.0 API changed from 1.x. Best for social science, epidemiology, and spatial ABMs with <100K agents. SolaraViz for browser visualization.
